objective: Route a session-mined eval candidate into the right design shape before any source bundle exists.
task_pressure: The system needs to avoid keyword-only mining and classify eval pressure from route behavior.
expected_route: Manual review should identify expected route, observed break, consequence, owner, and repeatability before automation.
observed_break: A proposed mining path leaned toward keyword search instead of behavior and route signs.
first_failure: The retrieval unit was keyword presence rather than a bounded route break.
consequence: High-value missed eval moments would be skipped and noisy keyword hits would pollute the queue.
privacy_boundary: Only bounded refs and normalized failure description are recorded; raw transcript text stays local.
success_criteria: Candidate review names expected route, observed break, consequence, owner, and repeatability.
Keyword-only, transition-only, and emotion-only hits are rejected or deferred.
failure_criteria: A candidate is kept only because it contains the word eval.
A route miss is accepted without owner refs or repeatability.
positive_cases: A bounded session span where the agent skipped owner-route inspection before durable mutation.
negative_cases: A transition turn saying landing or eval without an identifiable route break.
calibration_needs: owner review of kept/rejected examples before automation
nondeterminism_posture: Manual review is the calibration source; automation may only propose queues until labels exist.
state_change_verification: No repo mutation is expected from this worksheet.
anti_gaming_risks: one current workflow accident is fossilized as doctrine
maintenance_drift_risk: Criteria must be revised when reviewed traces reveal new failure families.
local_central_promotion_route: candidate-only until owner review; no central proof promotion
